title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: rome minimal R package read write multiscale OME-Zarr files. also provides helper methods manipulate resulting ome_zarr objects way one manipulate traditional arrays R. example, can subset ome_zarr object using [ operator, subsetting applied levels multiscale OME-Zarr object.

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: can install development version rome like :

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: basic example shows read OME-ZARR image version 0.4. default, read performed lazily using ZarrArray. Otherwise read can performed memory : remote OME-ZARR files, can use paws.storage::s3 client read data directly S3 bucket without downloading first:
code: library(rome) library(utils) omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata", "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip", package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(td) x #> Multiscale OME-Zarr image (v0.4) object. #> Scale: 1/5  #> <2 x 5 x 5> DelayedArray object of type "integer": #> ,,1 #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]   11   16    9   11   11 #> [2,]    9    7   10    6   11 #>  #> ,,2 #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]    2   11    8   11   10 #> [2,]   12    2    8   11   11 #>  #> ,,3 #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]   11    6   18    4    7 #> [2,]   12    8   13    6    9 #>  #> ,,4 #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]   13    8   17    6    3 #> [2,]    9    6    8    5    8 #>  #> ,,5 #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] #> [1,]   14   13    4   10    5 #> [2,]    9   11    6   12    6 x <- ome_read(td, lazy = FALSE) library(paws) s3_client <- paws.storage::s3(   config = list(     credentials = list(anonymous = TRUE),     region = "auto",     endpoint = "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk"   ) ) x <- ome_read(   "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0076A/10501752.zarr",    s3_client = s3_client, ) plot(x, all = TRUE)

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: Labels image pyramids can also read images
code: omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata", "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip", package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(file.path(td, "labels/blobs")) plot(x, all = TRUE)

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: rome also provides utilities writing OME-ZARR images OME-NGFF versions 0.4 0.5. Users can also define scaling factors image pyramids. scalefactors length three, pyramid four scales. Eac scale factor vector defines scale factor relative previous scale.
code: # read image library(EBImage) img_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_RGB.png", package="rome") img <- readImage(img_file)  # write image, version 0.4 ome_img <- ome_write(img,                      path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                      version = "0.4",                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64,64,1))) ome_img <- ome_write(img,                      path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                      version = "0.5",                       scalefactors = c(2,2,3),                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64,64,1)))

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: OME-ZARR label pyramids can generated way. first create label data using EBImage first. can now write image pyramid. Arguments similar labels.
code: # read image library(EBImage)  # read the first frame of image nuc <- readImage(system.file("images", "nuclei.tif", package="EBImage")) nuc <- getFrames(nuc)[[1]]  # threshold using otsu's method nuc_th = nuc > otsu(nuc) # write label, version 0.4 ome_nuc_th <- ome_write(nuc_th,                         path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                         version = "0.4",                         scalefactors = c(2,2,3),                         storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64,64)),                          type = "label") plot(ome_nuc_th, 3)

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
code: ome_read(path, s3_client = NULL, lazy = TRUE, validate = TRUE)

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
text: path Path OME-Zarr file. s3_client Object created paws.storage::s3(). required file S3. Leave NULL file local storage. lazy Logical. TRUE (default), use ZarrArray read data lazily. FALSE, read data memory using Rarr. data can fit memory, setting lazy = FALSE may result better performance. validate Logical.TRUE (default), validate OME-Zarr file.

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read

ome_write — ome_write
text: Writes ome image zarr path according ome-zarr specification

title: ome_write — ome_write
code: ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_metadata = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'character' ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_metadata = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'array' ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_metadata = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'Image' ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_metadata = NULL )

title: ome_write — ome_write
text: image n-dimensional (path ) array representing image data (1<n<6) path path writing ome.zarr axes character vector specifying axes image (e.g. c("t", "c", "z", "y", "x")) scalefactors Scale factors apply construct multiscale image. Importantly, scale factor relative previous scale factor. example, scale factors c(2, 2, 2), returned multiscale image 4 scales. version OME-ZARR version (0.4 0.5), lower versions supported writing. storage_options list storage options zarr array (e.g. chunks) type type OME pyramid written: 'image' (default) 'label'. label_metadata label metadata added attributes

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ x <- ome_read(   "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0076A/10501752.zarr" ) y <- x[3, 1:5, 1:5] extract_levels(y, 2) plot(y, level = 2) } # }
